A Guide to Payments in Cambodia and QR Code Etiquette for Local Banks| Cambodia Payment Guide: USD vs KHR _yoast_wpseo_metadesc Unveiling the Unspoken Rules of Payments in Cambodia: Why Are $100 Bills Unpopular? What Are the Key "Social Etiquette" Tips to Note When Using QR Code Payments via Local Banks like ABA and Acleda? _yoast_wpseo_linkdex 46 _yoast_wpseo_content_score 90 _yoast_wpseo_estimated-reading-time-minutes 1 rank_math_og_content_image a:2:{s:5:"check";s:32:"7b61bc266df67c1f093965509a7764d1";s:6:"images";a:1:{i:0;i:736;}} _wp_page_template default http://localhost/west-baray-siem-reap-hidden-gem/ Mon, 23 Mar 2026 03:00:00 +0000 xiakey https://www.camtravel.xyz/?p=738

      I’ll be honest. When I first saw the West Baray in Siem Reap, I thought, "Okay, it's just a big, ordinary-looking lake."

      Standing on the reddish-brown tiled embankment, all you see is a vast, calm body of water stretching to the horizon. There are no dramatic waterfalls or jagged cliffs. Just… water.

      It looks like an ocean, but it's actually a 1,000-year-old man-made reservoir.

      But then I learned the crazy facts. This is NOT a natural lake. It’s a reservoir, and it was entirely constructed by hand and elephant power back in the 11th century.

      To give you an idea of the scale, West Baray is roughly 8 kilometers long and 2.1 kilometers wide. That makes it about FIVE TIMES the size of New York’s Central Park.

      Imagine digging that with nothing but bamboo baskets to carry the earth. Suddenly, that "ordinary" water felt like a monumental achievement of ancient engineering.

      The embankment was entirely constructed by hand and elephant power in the 11th century.

      Today, the reservoir is still doing its job, providing water for local farming and acting as a peaceful escape for people in Siem Reap. You can see longtail boats tied up under the shade of ancient trees, and locals enjoying a quiet afternoon by the water.

      If you’re tired of the temple crowds at Angkor Wat, come here for the breeze. It might look ordinary at first glance, but the history beneath the surface is anything but.

      "Don't miss the 5x Central Park Marvel. Check out my visit to the massive West Baray."

      738 2026-03-23 10:00:00 2026-03-23 03:00:00 2026-03-28 12:23:53 2026-03-28 05:23:53 open open west-baray-siem-reap-hidden-gem publish 0 0 post 0 Cambodia Guides rank_math_internal_links_processed 1 rank_math_seo_score 15 _edit_last 1 _yoast_wpseo_primary_category _yoast_wpseo_focuskw West Baray Siem Reap _yoast_wpseo_title It Looks Ordinary, but West Baray is 5x Central Park _yoast_wpseo_metadesc Is it a lake? No, it's a 1,000-year-old reservoir 5x the size of Central Park. Discover why West Baray is the ultimate engineering marvel in Siem Reap. _yoast_wpseo_linkdex 73 _yoast_wpseo_content_score 90 _yoast_wpseo_estimated-reading-time-minutes 2 rank_math_og_content_image a:2:{s:5:"check";s:32:"14b3fc78aa72303c3c27be83f0208fef";s:6:"images";a:1:{i:0;i:739;}} _wp_page_template default http://localhost/zto-express-siem-reap-shipping-guide/ Mon, 23 Mar 2026 03:10:00 +0000 xiakey https://www.camtravel.xyz/?p=743

      Click the button below to go directly to the ZTO official tracking system. Enter the tracking number to track the shipment status.

      [Click here to check your order on the ZTO website]

      Updated May 2026: The ZTO Express station in Siem Reap is currently operating stably, and the operating rules are as follows:

      Moving to Siem Reap often means realizing that local international shipping can be incredibly expensive. While DHL and FedEx are reliable, their rates can be a shock to your budget. Enter ZTO Express (中通快递)—a game-changer for expats who want to ship goods from China (via Taobao or 1688) at a fraction of the cost.

      The ZTO Express office in Siem Reap is easy to spot with its distinctive blue and white branding.

        • 1. Why Choose ZTO Over DHL?

      The primary reason is cost. ZTO specializes in the China-Cambodia route. If you are ordering household items, electronics, or outdoor gear from China, ZTO offers a "consolidated shipping" service. You ship your items to their warehouse in China, and they bring them to Siem Reap for a much lower weight-based fee.

        • 2. How It Works: The "Taobao + ABA" Strategy

      Many locals and savvy expats use their ABA Bank app to pay directly on Taobao.

    • Set up Taobao: Use a translation tool to navigate.

    • Shipping Address: Set your shipping address to ZTO’s China transit warehouse (ask the Siem Reap office for their latest warehouse code).

    • Tracking: Once it arrives in Cambodia, you’ll get a notification via phone or the app. A busy day at the ZTO warehouse—a sign of how many people are using this affordable service.

       

        • 3. Picking Up Your Parcel in Siem Reap

      The ZTO collection point in Siem Reap is a hub of activity. You’ll often see a mix of locals and expats picking up everything from small parcels to large furniture.

    • Location: Most ZTO points are centrally located near the old market or main roads (National Highway 6).

    • What to bring: Just your phone with the tracking number or the SMS notification.

      Pro Tip: If you're heading to the ZTO Express office from another city, or planning your next trip after picking up your package, I recommend using 👉 Book Your Bus Tickets via 12Go. It saves you a trip to the station and ensures you get the best price online!

        • Final Thoughts — Navigating Life on the Red Earth

      Settling into Siem Reap (or anywhere in Cambodia) is an incredible adventure, but it comes with its own puzzle of practical logistics. Finding an affordable shipping lifeline like ZTO Express is just the first step to feeling at home.

      To truly master your local routine without the headaches, two other things are essential: staying connected seamlessly and managing your money without getting ripped off.

      For data, skipping the airport long lines and [getting an Airalo eSIM here] ensures you have reliable internet the second you arrive. And when it comes to paying shipping fees, handling rent, or converting currencies locally, traditional banks will eat your funds in hidden fees. I highly recommend using [this Wise link] to manage your transfers—it gives you the real exchange rate, and you’ll get a fee-free transfer of up to £500 to support your journey.

      "When picking up your package, there are many people and a lot of people around, so I suggest preparing a screenshot with the tracking number in advance. I usually create a 'Logistics' folder in my phone's photo album to store these pictures, so that I can communicate with the counter most efficiently."

      Take it one step at a time, and enjoy the rhythm of Cambodia!

We've also included Siem Reap branch addresses, contact numbers, and tips to avoid common pitfalls, saving you time and hassle! _yoast_wpseo_linkdex 62 _yoast_wpseo_content_score 60 _yoast_wpseo_estimated-reading-time-minutes 4 _wp_page_template default _oembed_aab5f41fe049557ca0758c68e88b9336 {{unknown}} _oembed_f3ae877e2619fcf01b40f49039416c7e {{unknown}} http://localhost/boost-guava-growth-cambodia-red-soil/ Fri, 27 Mar 2026 13:43:14 +0000 xiakey https://www.camtravel.xyz/?p=748

      The guava tree before intervention: Healthy green leaves, but stagnant growth

      Introduction | In tropical regions like Siem Reap, having green leaves doesn't always mean your tree is thriving. My air-layered guava tree looked healthy, but its growth had stagnated. The culprit? The iron-hard red soil of Cambodia that acts like a cage for young roots.These trial plants are my primary samples for testing how air-layered fruit trees adapt to the iron-rich, fast-draining red soil of Kampong Kdei

      H2: The Problem: Compact Soil & Stunted Roots

      The challenge: Hard red soil that restricts root expansion.

      The red earth here is notoriously compact. Without intervention, even a healthy sapling will struggle to expand its root system, leading to a "bonsai effect" where the tree stays small for years.

      H2: The Strategy: Feed the Soil, Not Just the Tree

      Adding dry cow manure to create a loose, nutrient-rich root zone.

      To break this cycle, I dug a wide perimeter around the root zone and (backfilled) it with dry cow manure. This organic amendment serves two purposes: providing slow-release nutrients and, more importantly, improving soil porosity so roots can finally "breathe" and move.

      H2: Strategic Pruning: The "Root-First" Sacrifice

      Strategic Pruning: Sacrificing leaves now for a stronger root system later.

      I then made a bold move: a heavy pruning that left only the main trunk. By removing the foliage, the plant no longer needs to spend energy on transpiration. Instead, it can redirect 100% of its resources to building a massive, dense root system underground.

      Conclusion | A year of top growth starts with a month of root growth. By improving the soil and pruning strategically, I'm setting this air-layered guava tree up for long-term success in the challenging Cambodian landscape.

      💡 More from CamTravel.xyz:

    • Tech & Connectivity: How I maintain a stable connection in remote areas using an [Industrial 4G Router].

Discover how to use organic manure and strategic pruning to invigorate healthy saplings in Cambodia's tough red earth. _yoast_wpseo_linkdex 71 _yoast_wpseo_content_score 60 _yoast_wpseo_estimated-reading-time-minutes 3 _wp_page_template default http://localhost/cellcard-4g-router-speed-test-rural-cambodia/ Fri, 27 Mar 2026 14:51:47 +0000 xiakey https://www.camtravel.xyz/?p=759

      Introduction | Based on my recent data from camtravel.xyz, many of you are interested in the practicalities of living in Cambodia. One of the biggest challenges is maintaining a stable connection while moving between places like Phnom Penh and Kampong Kdei. For me, the solution is simple: an industrial 4G router paired with Cellcard.

      H2: Stability Over Speed | When I'm uploading 1080p videos or managing the backend of this blog, I don't need 100Mbps fiber. I need a connection that doesn't drop. In my latest rural test, Cellcard delivered a steady 14.59 Mbps.

      My real-world test results (Chinese UI): 14.59 Mbps is more than enough for remote blog management.

      H2: Why Not Just a Phone Hotspot? Phones are great for browsing, but they struggle with heat and multi-device management during long work sessions. My plug-in router handles the tropical heat of Cambodia far better and keeps my computer and phones connected simultaneously without lag.

      🔗 Stay Connected with CamTravel:

    • Rural Living: How I used this connection to manage my [Guava Tree Invigoration Project].

    • Historical Sites: Read my deep dive into the history of the [Ancient Spean Praptos Bridge].

    • In addition to mastering remote connectivity in the provinces, surviving in rural Cambodia also requires understanding local social protocols—like the 80,000 Riel banquet rule.

My real-world Cellcard 4G speed test using an industrial router. 14Mbps stable connection for content creators. _yoast_wpseo_linkdex 65 _yoast_wpseo_content_score 90 _yoast_wpseo_estimated-reading-time-minutes 2 rank_math_og_content_image a:2:{s:5:"check";s:32:"72b7b6407abb1006f632cb51a52cbe9d";s:6:"images";a:1:{i:0;i:760;}} _wp_page_template default http://localhost/spean-praptos-the-ghost-bridges-a-motorbike-field-report-from-route-6/ Sun, 29 Mar 2026 17:27:50 +0000 xiakey https://www.camtravel.xyz/?p=781

      • Foreword

      Many ask why I bother revisiting places in 2026. The answer lies within my "silently dead" smartphone—a victim of motherboard failure earlier this year. While I lost my high-definition archives, the loss taught me that digital assets are fragile. Today, standing by the ancient stones of Kampong Kdei, I am not just replacing lost photos; I am documenting the intersection of ancient and modern infrastructure with fresh eyes.

      • Authentic Disclosure

      The Geometric Aesthetics Spanning a Millennium: The Interior of Spean Praptos Bridge Hole

      100% Grounded. AI-Refined. All content is based on my personal field trips in Cambodia and Laos. I provide the core logic and raw materials; AI polishes the language to ensure clarity for global readers. Truth is the only priority.

      • H1: The Micro-details of a Millennium Bridge

      Spean Praptos is more than a bridge; it’s a survivor of the Angkor era. Touching these stones with visible chisel marks, you can feel the pulse of craftsmen from a thousand years ago. While official records state the bridge has 21 arches, I was too busy seeking shade within their cool geometric shadows to count them one by one. This imperfection is the true charm of field research—it always leaves a reason to return.

      The wonder without adhesive: Stone blocks from a thousand years ago still fit tightly together. Personal verification: These mosses and textures bear witness to the actual temperature of this place.

      Walking through the stone slits into the arches, light turns to shadow. The cool sensation and geometric weight are things static photos can hardly capture.

      • H2：The Ghost Bridges of Route 6

      True exploration often happens beyond the view of an express bus. Along the old sections of Route 6 near Kampong Kdei, I spotted two or three other fragmentary ancient bridges. Passing them frequently on my motorbike, these ruins have become familiar landmarks. It’s heartening that local road construction teams did not demolish them; instead, they chose to divert the new asphalt.

      Modern civilization has chosen to take a detour: the "ghost bridge" beside the new road Forgotten Name: Spean Thmar - Old Bridge Significance

      • H3： Ancient Hub Meets Modern Finance

      The anchor point of modern finance: The ACLEDA Bank, several hundred meters away from the ancient bridge. This is the most reliable physical bank branch in the local area.

      Interestingly, just a few hundred meters from this ancient relic sits ACLEDA Bank. In Cambodia, ACLEDA represents tangible trust. Regrettably, after hours in the sun, I remembered the 5,000 Riel note features this very bridge, but I had already left. Though the doors are closed this Sunday, the security provided by its physical presence is something no virtual bank can match.

      Now that you've seen the resilience of ancient stones, check out my post on [West Baray], another story about loss and reconstruction.

A motorbike field report on ancient stones and modern banking in Kampong Kdei, Cambodia. _yoast_wpseo_linkdex 64 _yoast_wpseo_content_score 90 _yoast_wpseo_estimated-reading-time-minutes 4 _wp_page_template default http://localhost/cambodia-village-banquet-social-etiquette-80000-riel/ Mon, 30 Mar 2026 02:45:49 +0000 xiakey https://www.camtravel.xyz/?p=796

       

      The iconic pink tent: A signal of high-standard social events in rural Cambodia.

      Just a few hundred meters from the 12th-century Spean Praptos bridge, the modern pulse of Cambodia beats inside a vibrant pink silk tent. In March 2026, while traveling along Route 6 near Kampong Kdei, I was invited to a local "Birthday Banquet"—an event that, in scale and splendor, rivaled many Western weddings.

      https://youtu.be/81OQTeby5n0

      The thumping rhythm of the party: Where ancient history meets 2026 celebration.

      • H1:The "Invitation Bomb" and the Cash Envelope

      In rural Cambodia, social life is governed by what many expats call the "Invitation Bomb"—a physical, high-quality card delivered to your door that carries the weight of a reciprocal social obligation. Even if you cannot attend, the "gift" is often expected.

      For this 2026 village gathering, the protocol was clear: 80,000 Khmer Riel (approx. $20 USD). I prepared the cash and sealed the red envelope right at the entrance, where the hosts greeted guests with traditional Khmer hospitality.

      • H2: Why Riel Matters in Local Ceremonies

      High-standard hospitality: Even in remote villages, the banquet decor is impeccable

      While US Dollars dominate the cities, the Cambodian Riel is the language of respect in village ceremonies. Using 80,000 Riel instead of a $20 bill is a subtle mark of traditional etiquette. It shows you aren't just a passerby, but someone who understands the local social fabric.

      💡 Real Cambodia Beyond the Resorts: Want to experience authentic Khmer village life, meet local families, and see how rural traditions thrive outside the tourist zones? The safest and most respectful way to explore is with an expert local guide. You can search through dozens of curated itineraries on Viator's Siem Reap Cultural Experiences to find a local village tour that perfectly matches your travel style.

      Want to dig deeper into Khmer social life?

    • [Related]: The Survival Guide to Khmer "Invitation Bombs"

Learn the 80,000 Riel social protocol, invitation etiquette, and real-time social observation from Route 6. _yoast_wpseo_linkdex 69 _yoast_wpseo_content_score 60 _yoast_wpseo_estimated-reading-time-minutes 3 _wp_page_template default http://localhost/se-asia-financial-evolution-2018-2022-2026-bcel-wing-acleda/ Mon, 30 Mar 2026 13:42:25 +0000 xiakey https://www.camtravel.xyz/?p=804

      (The 8-Year Legacy)

      • H1: Introduction

      Opening this BCEL Lao passbook issued in 2018, my thoughts drift back to Vientiane. In early 2022, I spent about $300 on a Vietnam Airlines ticket, flying from Vientiane to Phnom Penh with these physical vouchers and my savings. Over 8 years, financial tools have shifted, but my logic of "redundancy" has remained constant.

      • H2：Wing: The Practicality of Being Unbanked

      Arriving in Cambodia in early 2022, roadside Wing agents became my economic lifeline. I still remember the practicality of remitting funds via a POS terminal with a 10,000 Riel fee and just a phone number. Though localized, this was the most reliable support for an expat during that digital gap.

      • H3: ACLEDA: Digital and Local Dominance

      Today, I use the ACLEDA Bank app, featuring multi-language support (Khmer, English, Lao, Chinese) and seamless QR integration with ABA. ACLEDA maintains a massive physical presence here. While I enjoy the speed of digital payments, I still keep my Lao passbook. In SE Asia, advanced digital integration is for efficiency, but primitive physical vouchers are the ultimate baseline.

       

      • 🧳 CamTravel's Cross-Border Toolkit

      If you are retracing these steps or building your own redundancy plan in Southeast Asia, here are the platforms I trust for structural reliability:

      • Flights & Regional Transits: Secure your cross-border routes via [Trip.com].

      • Digital Connectivity Backup: Avoid the digital gap with [Airalo eSIM].

      • Mid-to-Long Term Accommodations: Base yourself securely using [Expedia].

From my 2018 BCEL "Lao Blue" backup to flying with Vietnam Airlines in 2022 and modern ACLEDA digital payments. _yoast_wpseo_linkdex 60 _yoast_wpseo_content_score 90 _yoast_wpseo_estimated-reading-time-minutes 2 _wp_page_template default rank_math_og_content_image a:2:{s:5:"check";s:32:"3b152b8c09036bf10bd451bceeb22b10";s:6:"images";a:1:{i:0;s:84:"http://localhost/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/图片_20260404113216_837_6-300x202.webp";}} rank_math_contentai_score a:5:{s:8:"keywords";s:5:"74.51";s:9:"wordCount";s:1:"0";s:9:"linkCount";s:1:"0";s:12:"headingCount";s:1:"0";s:10:"mediaCount";s:1:"0";} http://localhost/6-month-summary-workbench-repairman-to-orchardist-cambodia/ Mon, 30 Mar 2026 15:10:47 +0000 xiakey https://www.camtravel.xyz/?p=810

      • H1：6 Months of Bytes and Soil

      In late September 2025, I purchased CamTravel.xyz and began recording under challenging hardware and network conditions. Six months later, I’ve evolved from an observer to a field experimenter. My workbench (Image 1) displays this evolution: from a soldering iron for appliance repairs, to bank passbooks auditing 8 years of history, to pruning shears for fruit tree grafting.

      H2: Video Genesis

      My video documentation began in August 2025. The YouTube channel CamTravel Field Reports captured early pet videos. Despite having only 3 subscribers then, those 1,000+ views proved the inherent appeal of authentic life.

      H3: The Lemon Project & Community

      I plan to graft lemon scions onto two struggling citrus rootstocks. This opportunity stems from community goodwill built through voluntary electronics repairs. Despite the challenge of solo recording, this fieldwork remains our core mission.

      ⚖️ (Copyright Notice)

From electronics repair to guava pruning and the upcoming lemon grafting project by Route 6. _yoast_wpseo_linkdex 60 _yoast_wpseo_content_score 90 _yoast_wpseo_estimated-reading-time-minutes 2 rank_math_og_content_image a:2:{s:5:"check";s:32:"39b29a79df70c94a2da8432336b43cd7";s:6:"images";a:1:{i:0;s:58:"http://localhost/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/2-1024x768.jpg";}} http://localhost/iy-mosquito-swatter-18650-type-c-upgrade/ Tue, 31 Mar 2026 09:35:27 +0000 xiakey https://www.camtravel.xyz/?p=822

      H1: (Introduction)

      The teardown: Preparing to transplant the 18650 battery.

      In rural Cambodia, headlamps are essential for every household due to the lack of streetlights at night. Recently, my electric mosquito swatter became useless after the 220V charging pins broke and the lead-acid battery died. Instead of throwing it away, I decided to give it a second life using a 18650 lithium battery from a scrapped headlamp.

      Safety first: Always keep the original protection board when transplanting lithium batteries. Goodbye 220V: Now I can charge it anywhere with a smartphone cable.

      H2: Steps

    • (Scrapping): Retrieve functional 18650 lithium batteries from scrapped headlamps.

    • (Port Upgrade): Discard the original cumbersome 220V plug, add a Type-C female port, and reinforce the structure using 401 adhesive with PCB scraps.

    • (Wiring): Connect the battery protection board to the circuit to ensure stable lithium battery output.

    • (Testing): The high discharge efficiency of the lithium battery significantly increases the electric shock power, producing a crisper sound and delivering substantial "lethality."

      Copyright Notice: All text and photos in this article are original by CamTravel.xyz. All rights reserved.

      • Internal Links:

      If you are interested in practical survival skills in Cambodia, you might want to check out my other articles:

    • [Preliminary preparations for lemon tree grafting: a guide to tools and material selection.]

      822 2026-03-31 16:35:27 2026-03-31 09:35:27 2026-04-04 07:45:30 2026-04-04 00:45:30 open open iy-mosquito-swatter-18650-type-c-upgrade publish 0 0 post 0 Cambodia Guides rank_math_internal_links_processed 1 rank_math_seo_score 15 _edit_last 1 _yoast_wpseo_primary_category _yoast_wpseo_focuskw 18650 Electric Mosquito Swatter DIY 18650 _yoast_wpseo_title DIY Upgrade: 18650 & Type-C for Mosquito Swatter _yoast_wpseo_metadesc DIY upgrade: Reviving a mosquito swatter with 18650 battery & Type-C charging in Cambodia. _yoast_wpseo_linkdex 62 _yoast_wpseo_content_score 90 _yoast_wpseo_estimated-reading-time-minutes 2 rank_math_og_content_image a:2:{s:5:"check";s:32:"21d5d339ad994bfbcab13476b16dc48a";s:6:"images";a:1:{i:0;s:85:"http://localhost/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/图片_20260331160244_813_6-767x1024.webp";}} http://localhost/siem-reap-zto-express-water-filter-shipping/ Wed, 01 Apr 2026 02:30:00 +0000 xiakey https://www.camtravel.xyz/?p=827

       

      • H1. (Introduction)

      Living in Cambodia, water quality is always a topic you can't ignore. While the well water along National Road 6 is inexhaustible, occasional turbidity and heavy limescale are real headaches. Instead of buying bottled water daily, I prefer a low-cost DIY solution through cross-border logistics.

      After 10+ days across China and Vietnam, finally arrived in Siem Reap.

       

      • H2. (Logistics)

      Many wonder: is shipping a $17 water purifier internationally expensive? The actual cost to ZTO Siem Reap was $5. The process is clear: domestic collection -> customs clearance -> Vietnam transit -> ZTO Siem Reap. It took about 10-15 days. Although shipping was nearly 30% of the item's value, it's still far cheaper than the $50-$100 options found locally.

       

       

      • H3. (Installation & Result)

      A $17 filter solving the drinking water issue on National Road 6.

      Unboxing, drilling, and mounting it on the concrete wall. This 6-stage ultrafiltration system lacks a fancy shell but excels in utility. With PP cotton, carbon, and the UF membrane working together, the water is finally clear. It's much safer now for cooking and direct drinking.

      "After finishing with the courier and installation, my stomach started protesting. I remembered the grouper and roast duck I had in Phnom Penh a couple of days ago. Although that meal cost a hundred bucks, compared to the hard-installed water purifier, life is like that: save where you can (DIY repairs), and spend where it's worth (an occasional big meal as a reward)."

      The best reward after hard work: authentic Chinese cuisine. Grouper head used in a sour cabbage fish soup.

      ZTO International has a highly established logistics network in Southeast Asia. Beyond this $17 water filter, my other cross-border packages (as shown below) consistently follow the same proven route: Guangzhou Warehouse -> Export Customs Clearance -> Vietnam Transit -> Phnom Penh Sorting Center -> National Road 6, Siem Reap. This unwavering stability is the primary reason I recommend their service.

      [caption id="attachment_859" align="alignnone" width="139"] ZTO Express shipping route: Guangzhou - Vietnam - Phnom Penh (example)[/caption]

      • ⚖️ (Copyright Notice)

However, as **March is the dry season in Cambodia**, expect strong winds. Sand can easily blow into your eyes; **it is highly recommended to wear cycling glasses or sunglasses** and be mindful of crosswinds.&nbsp;🎬 H3：A firsthand account of the intersection of Siem Reap Airport and Highway 6 leading to the edge of the city. <a href="https://youtu.be/Cyzn8Jlpm6Y">https://youtu.be/Cyzn8Jlpm6Y</a>&nbsp;(Copyright)© 2026 CamTravel.xyz.
